title: Listener gets stale CLAUDE_SESSION_ID when Monitor-spawned process spans /clear
description:
## Symptom
After \`/clear\` and a new \`/converse:converse\`, speak.py's session-id check fails — lock file contains a different session_id than the current Stop-hook payload. TTS is silently skipped.

## Root cause
The listener process captures \`CLAUDE_SESSION_ID\` from its launch env at execve and writes it to the lock file. Monitor-spawned processes have frozen env for their entire lifetime. Even when a new /converse starts a new listener, Claude Code's own env may still carry an older session_id (the SessionStart → CLAUDE_ENV_FILE chain has gaps for long-running contexts), and the new listener writes that older id to the lock file.

## Fix
PreToolUse hook that intercepts the tool call launching the listener, sources session_id directly from its own hook payload (Claude Code's authoritative value, no env var chain), and prepends \`CLAUDE_SESSION_ID=<id>\` to the command via \`updatedInput\`. The listener then sees the correct session_id in its env and writes it to the lock file as before.

This fixes the root cause without changing speak.py / render_status.py.

## Acceptance
- After /clear + /converse, lock file session_id matches the current session.
- speak.py honors the check correctly in both old and new sessions.

title: Whisper persistent-state hallucinations: set --no-context on whisper-server
description:
## Symptom
Whisper enters a state where it keeps outputting similar wrong phrases regardless of what the user actually says. Persists until voicemode restarts the whisper service. User-noticeable as: \"whisper has gone stale\".

## Root cause
whisper-server defaults to keeping and reusing the previous audio context across requests (\`--max-context -1\` = unlimited). When one request produces a hallucination or repetition loop, the garbage becomes context for the next request, biasing it toward the same garbage. It cascades and survives until the server is restarted (the cache resets).

Confirmed from \`whisper-server --help\`:
- \`-mc N, --max-context N [-1]\` — max context tokens (default unlimited)
- \`-nc, --no-context\` — do not use previous audio context

Current voicemode-managed startup (from \`ps\`):
\`whisper-server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 2022 --model ggml-base.bin --inference-path /v1/audio/transcriptions --threads 8\`

No context-management flags set → vulnerable.

## Fix
Add \`--no-context\` to voicemode's whisper-server startup script.

Each transcription in this project is a discrete utterance from VAD — there is no reason to carry decoder context from the previous utterance into the next. \`--no-context\` makes each request independent; a bad transcription can no longer poison future ones.

## Investigate / do
- Find where voicemode spawns whisper-server (\`~/.voicemode/services/whisper/\` area or config).
- Add \`--no-context\` to that invocation.
- Also consider: \`--suppress-nst\` (drop non-speech tokens), \`--no-fallback\` (don't use temperature fallback, which can make repetition loops worse), \`-nth 0.6\` is default no_speech_thold — may want to raise.
- Restart whisper-server, validate stale-state bug no longer reproduces.

## Acceptance
- Bad transcriptions no longer cascade into subsequent unrelated utterances.
- No whisper-service restart required to recover from a one-off hallucination.
close_reason: Fix documented: whisper-server needs --no-context. External change in voicemode install applied and validated; setup note + SKILL.md update committed.

title: Replace --- echo/response delimiter with [heard]/[/heard] wrapper
description: The current Stop hook splits echo from response on '---', but '---' is a common markdown construct (horizontal rule, table separator, YAML frontmatter, diff markers). When a response contains one, TTS truncates at the wrong point or misses entirely. Replace the delimiter with a byte-0-anchored BBCode-style wrapper [heard]...[/heard]; the BBCode shape is not HTML so the terminal renderer keeps it visible, and the echo content between markers remains visible too. Parser fails open (speak the whole message) on any missing/malformed wrapper.
acceptance_criteria:
- hooks/speak.py strip_echo_prefix: byte-0 anchored; strip [heard]...[/heard] block and trailing blanks; fail-open on any malformed input
- skills/converse/SKILL.md: echo-protocol section uses [heard]/[/heard]; no mention of --- delimiter
- Plugin version bumped to 0.6.1 in both .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
- Tests cover: complete response, partial input (Shape B), no-wrapper input, unclosed opener, opener not at byte 0, [/heard] appearing in response body
notes: Design history in session: rejected out-of-band match (Claude rewrites noisy transcriptions), rejected structural blockquote detection (fails closed), rejected tool-call approach (blocking semantics contradict plugin purpose), rejected inverted 'speak fence' (fails silent not fail-open). See also claude-converse-6tg (daemon consolidation) which will absorb this parser unchanged.

title: Extract VAD state machine and add tests
description:
The listener's VAD logic is an untested 140-line state machine inside run() (skills/converse/listener.py:222-359). Tuning thresholds today means "listen and see if it feels right" — no way to encode a specific RMS sequence that misbehaves and verify the fix.

## Proposed sequence

1. **Mechanical extraction**: move the 8 state locals (speech_frames, consecutive_speech, consecutive_silence, consecutive_loud, in_speech, chunk_emitted, tts_killed, utterance_id, pre_buffer) into a VadState dataclass. Also pack the 9 tuning constants into a VadConfig with from_env(). No behavior change.

2. **Add unit tests** against the extracted state. Mock the three side effects (kill_tts, _emit, print). Cover:
   - Short burst of noise doesn't trigger utterance
   - Three consecutive speech frames start utterance
   - Chunk emits at CHUNK_SILENCE_FRAMES; final emits at UTTERANCE_END_FRAMES
   - Barge-in needs sustained loudness; resets after quiet frame
   - Short utterance (< MIN_UTTERANCE_FRAMES) discarded
   - chunk_emitted fires only once per silence streak

   Property tests (hypothesis) for invariants: utterance_id monotonic, emit_final always follows utterance_started, state resets after final.

3. **Optional**: further refactor into a pure step(state, rms, config) -> FrameEvent function. Nice-to-have; tests from step 2 still catch regressions if you skip this.

## Why this matters

- Future threshold tuning becomes evidence-based instead of vibes-based
- Refactor is safe once tests are in place (currently high-risk because no test harness)
- Catches regressions like "breath too loud sometimes triggers barge-in" by encoding the exact frame sequence

## Acceptance

- VadState dataclass in place; run() is thinner
- pytest suite covers the scenarios above; passes on current behavior
- At least a handful of property tests with hypothesis

## Notes

Don't do this without tests — unverified refactoring of a subtle state machine is where regressions hide. The tests are doing the work; the refactor just reduces test boilerplate.

title: Write a README.md for claude-converse
description:
The repo has CLAUDE.md (for AI agents) but no README (for humans browsing GitHub).

## Sections to cover
- Title + one-sentence tagline (non-blocking, interruptible voice interface for Claude Code)
- What it is / what problem it solves
- Demo or usage GIF placeholder
- Requirements: \`voicemode\` (or compatible) running Whisper STT + Kokoro TTS locally
- Installation via Claude Code plugin marketplace (\`/plugin install converse@nafg/claude-converse\` or however the install works)
- Required setup notes:
  - whisper-server must be started with \`--no-context\` (link to CLAUDE.md \"Whisper setup requirement\")
  - Statusline integration one-liner (link to CLAUDE.md \"Statusline integration\")
- Usage: \`/converse on\`, \`/converse off\`, barge-in works by speaking over TTS
- How it works (brief): listener with energy VAD → Whisper → Monitor events → Claude → Stop hook → Kokoro → PyAudio
- Customization: env vars (VAD thresholds, KOKORO_VOICE, WHISPER_INITIAL_PROMPT, etc.)
- Troubleshooting (\`voicemode service restart whisper|kokoro\`)
- License (pick one)

## Acceptance
README.md committed, renders well on GitHub, and gives a stranger enough context to install and use the plugin without reading CLAUDE.md.

title: Status line shows voice transcriptions in other sessions (no session-id check)
description:
render_status.py only checks whether the lock file is held to decide whether to render. It doesn't check which session owns the lock, so the voice transcription scrollback shows up in the status line of every active Claude Code session, not just the one that ran /converse.

Fix: compare the lock file's content (session_id of the voice-owning session, written by listener.py) to render_status.py's own \$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID. Return empty if they don't match.

title: Pass conversation-specific initial_prompt to Whisper via listener arg
description:
## Why
Whisper's \`prompt\` parameter (initial_prompt) primes the decoder with expected vocabulary, which is the cheapest, most targeted way to reduce domain mis-hears and hallucinations. Currently \`listener.py\` sends no prompt, so Whisper has no hint that the speaker is talking about programming, a specific stack, or the current task.

## Design
- \`listener.py\` reads an initial prompt from an env var (e.g. \`WHISPER_INITIAL_PROMPT\`) and passes it as \`prompt\` in the \`/v1/audio/transcriptions\` request.
- The \`/converse\` skill generates an appropriate prompt based on the live conversation context (project language, technologies in play, file paths or terms recently discussed, current subsystem) and exports the env var before (re)starting the listener.
- Should be possible to refresh the prompt mid-session — e.g., restart the listener when context shifts significantly, or accept a control-channel update.

## Acceptance
- \`listener.py\` respects \`WHISPER_INITIAL_PROMPT\`.
- The skill fills a sensible, dynamic prompt per conversation.
- Measurable reduction in domain mis-hears on programming terms.
close_reason: Shipped: listener.py honors WHISPER_INITIAL_PROMPT; SKILL.md guides Claude to fill it per conversation.

title: Upgrade Whisper model above base (small.en / medium.en) with HDD-only storage
description:
## Why
The base model is producing well-known failure modes in real use:
- Repetition loops at end of utterance (\"We're going to... We're going to... We're going to...\")
- Domain mis-hears: \"worktree\" → \"service\", \"branch\" → \"Rebus\", random word salad like \"Spiriture\"

Upgrading to small.en or medium.en typically eliminates most of these issues for English dictation.

## Constraint
The SSD partition is nearly full. Any model downloaded by voicemode must land on the HDD, not the SSD.

Currently voicemode stores models at \`~/.voicemode/services/whisper/models/\` — need to verify which disk this is on and whether it can be redirected.

## Investigate / do
- Determine current model-directory disk (SSD vs HDD).
- Check whether voicemode supports a config / env var to redirect the model download path, or whether a symlink of \`~/.voicemode/services/whisper/models\` to an HDD location works.
- Benchmark small.en and medium.en on 8-thread CPU — are they fast enough for chunked streaming?
- Swap the model in whisper-server startup and verify quality improvement.

## Acceptance
- Larger model in use, stored on HDD.
- Transcription quality measurably better on programming terms.
- No SSD space pressure.
notes: HDD constraint is already satisfied: `~/.voicemode` lives on `/mnt/data` (/dev/sda2, 351 GB, 66 GB free) — the HDD. No special handling needed; voicemode will download larger models there by default.

title: Bundle status-line render routine with plugin
description: The user's status-line script lives outside the plugin, but the rendering logic (reading RECENT_FILE, applying the time window, formatting) should be bundled with the plugin. Expose a script like render_status.py that the user's status-line script can just invoke. Keeps the plugin the source of truth for how recent transcriptions are formatted.
close_reason: New skills/converse/render_status.py in the plugin — checks the lock file, filters the JSONL recent file by time window, formats with prefix+separator. CLI/env configurable. statusline.fish updated to locate and invoke it via ~/.claude/plugins/cache/claude-converse/converse/*/skills/converse/render_status.py.

title: Status-line window should be time-based not line-count-based
description: Currently RECENT_MAX_LINES=3 — always shows last 3 transcriptions regardless of age. Should instead show transcriptions from the last N seconds (e.g. 30s), expiring older entries. Requires storing timestamps alongside text in the recent file.
close_reason: Recent file switched from claude-converse-recent.txt (plain lines) to claude-converse-recent.jsonl ({id,final,ts,text} per line). render_status.py filters by --window-seconds (default 30, env CONVERSE_STATUS_WINDOW) instead of a fixed line count.

title: Decouple status-line update cadence from AI prompting
description: Currently transcriptions hit the status-line recent file and stdout (→ AI) at the same moment — when SILENCE_END_FRAMES is reached. If we raise the silence threshold for the AI, the status line will feel sluggish. Decouple: emit to recent file on a shorter silence gap (or continuously during speech), but only print to stdout/AI on the longer silence.
close_reason: Two silence thresholds: VAD_CHUNK_SILENCE_FRAMES (~600ms) triggers a non-final transcription that updates the recent file only, and VAD_UTTERANCE_END_FRAMES (~1500ms) triggers a final transcription that also goes to stdout (AI). Status line now updates ~900ms before the AI sees the prompt.

title: Make barge-in less eager
description: User reports TTS is interrupted when they weren't really talking (breath, background noise). Currently any audio above ENERGY_THRESHOLD for 3 frames (~90ms) kills TTS. Decouple barge-in from utterance-start: require higher energy (e.g. 2x threshold) and/or more frames (~180ms+) specifically for kill_tts(). User mentioned possibly volume-based.
close_reason: Barge-in decoupled from utterance-start. Now requires BARGE_IN_FRAMES (~180ms, default 6) consecutive frames with RMS > ENERGY_THRESHOLD * BARGE_IN_ENERGY_MULT (default 2.0). Fires at most once per loud streak; resets when streak ends so coughs before any utterance don't permanently disable future barge-ins.

title: Increase VAD silence-end threshold
description: User reports the listener prompts the AI too quickly after a pause. Currently SILENCE_END_FRAMES=30 (~900ms). Should be longer so natural thinking pauses don't trigger a response. Propose bumping to ~50 frames (~1500ms), possibly env-configurable.
close_reason: SILENCE_END_FRAMES renamed to UTTERANCE_END_FRAMES, bumped to 50 (~1500ms). All VAD thresholds are now env-configurable (VAD_THRESHOLD, VAD_SPEECH_START_FRAMES, VAD_CHUNK_SILENCE_FRAMES, VAD_UTTERANCE_END_FRAMES, VAD_MIN_UTTERANCE_FRAMES, VAD_PRE_BUFFER_FRAMES).

title: Consolidate voice-mode scripts into a single long-running daemon
description: The plugin currently fragments voice-mode state and behavior across multiple short-lived scripts that coordinate through the filesystem (lock files, PID files, ad-hoc log lines). This causes duplication (e.g. 'is voice session on' logic exists in both bash and Python), latency (Python startup per statusline invocation), and ownership ambiguity across the ~5 callsites. Consolidate into a single long-running daemon started by '/converse on', with thin IPC clients for each hook and the statusline.
design:
Open decisions to revisit when this work starts:

1. Language: Python (port existing code, low risk) vs Scala (bigger lift; requires audio capture via Java Sound API; may not be worth the rewrite vs incremental cleanup).

2. IPC transport: Unix domain socket + JSON-lines (recommended default -- standard, local-only, fast) vs HTTP (familiar but heavyweight) vs plain-text commands.

3. Daemon lifecycle: '/converse on/off' spawn/kill directly (simplest, matches current model) vs systemd user service (auto-restart, opt-in) vs lazy-start on first client RPC.

4. Supervision / restart policy if the daemon crashes mid-session.

5. Migration staging: big-bang rewrite vs keep current scripts as thin shims during transition, replacing one callsite at a time.

6. Naming: single 'converse' binary dispatching on subcommand ('converse daemon', 'converse client speak', 'converse client status') vs separate binaries per role ('conversed', 'converse-speak', etc.).

Ruled out during design discussion:
- Out-of-band echo-match (daemon uses transcription ground truth to strip): rejected because Claude cleans up weird transcriptions, so stored raw text and echoed blockquote diverge too often for either exact or fuzzy match to be reliable.
notes:
Current scripts to subsume:
- skills/converse/listener.py (mic + VAD + Whisper STT; already long-running via Monitor)
- hooks/speak.py (Stop hook: TTS via Kokoro; includes strip_echo_prefix, kill_existing_tts, mark_if_new, is_voice_session)
- skills/converse/render_status.py (statusline renderer)
- skills/converse/statusline-line.sh (bash wrapper that exists only to avoid Python startup cost)
- hooks/inject-session-id.py (PreToolUse hook: injects authoritative session_id)

Thin clients would replace each callsite with a short IPC message:
- Stop hook: {action: 'speak', msg: ..., session_id: ...}
- Statusline: {action: 'status', session_id: ...} -> one-line render or empty
- PreToolUse: {action: 'claim_session', session_id: ...}
- /converse on/off: start/stop the daemon
- Listener internal (barge-in): {action: 'kill_tts'}

Benefits:
- Eliminates filesystem-based shared state (lock files, PID files)
- Eliminates logic duplication across bash/python
- Statusline client becomes a short socket write -- no per-call Python startup, so the bash wrapper can disappear
- Naming coherence ('listener' noun + 'speak' verb currently not parallel; one module becomes one grammatical unit)
- Single source of truth for voice-session ownership
- Echo-stripping still lives in the daemon (same logic as a standalone hook would run), but its callers are unified

Out of scope here (tracked separately):
- The in-band echo-delimiter problem. That fix is independent and will land first; it'll just be relocated into the daemon when this work happens.

title: Create git tags + GitHub releases for past versions
description:
We've shipped 0.1.0 through 0.5.1 with no tags and no GitHub releases. Catch up.

## Version → bump commit
- 0.2.0 → 62b2f30
- 0.3.0 → 517f008
- 0.4.0 → ccb6bc6
- 0.4.1 → be1e479
- 0.5.0 → d5b6f70
- 0.5.1 → f388161

(0.1.0 was the initial commit; tag at \`20f3198\` if it counts as a release, or skip and start tagging from 0.2.0.)

## Steps
1. \`git tag -a vX.Y.Z <commit-sha> -m \"vX.Y.Z\"\` for each
2. \`git push --tags\`
3. \`gh release create vX.Y.Z --generate-notes\` — auto-generates notes from commits between previous tag and this one. \`--notes-start-tag\` lets you control the diff range.

## Going forward
Tag and release at every version bump from now on. Probably worth a tiny script or git alias.

## Acceptance
GitHub Releases page shows entries for every shipped version with non-empty release notes.

title: Set GitHub repo description and topics
description:
The repo has empty description and no topics on GitHub. Use \`gh repo edit\` to set:

- Description: short one-liner (e.g. \"Non-blocking, interruptible voice interface for Claude Code with local Whisper STT and Kokoro TTS\")
- Topics: claude-code, claude-code-plugin, voice, tts, stt, whisper, kokoro, voice-assistant, accessibility (pick a sensible subset)
- Optionally homepage URL

## Acceptance
\`gh repo view nafg/claude-converse\` shows a non-empty description and a sensible list of topics.

title: Status line should still show a mic indicator when voice mode is active but no recent transcription
description:
The 30-second window is intentional: after silence, the recent-transcription text disappears. But there's no indicator left that voice mode is active at all. The user has to remember.

Fix: if voice mode is active for this session but no transcription within the window, still emit just the prefix (e.g. \"🎤\") — small footprint, persistent affordance.

title: Evaluate streaming STT (whisper_streaming / WhisperLive) to replace batch whisper-server
description:
## Why
Current transcription is batch-at-pause, not streaming. Each chunk/final fires only after a silence boundary, and each call re-uploads the full accumulated utterance audio — so partial-update cost grows quadratically with utterance length. True streaming would:

- Deliver partials as words are spoken (~300 ms lag) instead of only at pauses
- Scale linearly in CPU regardless of utterance length
- Make the status line feel live rather than blocky

## Landscape
- **Cheap band-aid (rejected):** crank chunk cadence down to ~300 ms. Still batch; O(n²) CPU in utterance length.
- **Sliding-window streaming (the approach to evaluate):** projects like **whisper_streaming** (UFAL / Dominik Macháček, built on faster-whisper) and **WhisperLive** maintain a rolling buffer, transcribe only the tail each tick, commit words that stabilize across multiple passes, and re-transcribe only the unstable recent audio. WebSocket protocol typically.
- **Cloud (out of scope):** Deepgram / AssemblyAI / Google give sub-100 ms partials but cost money, go off-box, raise privacy concerns.

## Investigate / do
- Compare whisper_streaming, WhisperLive, and any other local streaming wrappers for faster-whisper.
- Install / run the best candidate locally; measure latency (first-partial, word-lag) and accuracy vs. the current base + \`--no-context\` setup.
- Decide whether to:
  a) Replace voicemode's whisper-server entirely (simpler but fights voicemode).
  b) Run the streaming service in parallel on a different port and have listener.py target it.
  c) Upstream a voicemode contribution so other users benefit.
- Design the client side: listener streams mic audio over WebSocket rather than batching WAV uploads. Barge-in still needs to kill TTS on speech start — keep the local VAD loop for that, just split audio publication from transcription triggering.

## Acceptance
- A decision document / note captured in this issue's notes comparing at least two streaming options with measured latency.
- If we proceed: a working prototype where partials appear on the status line within ~500 ms of speaking.

title: Configure status-line refresh interval (1s) via plugin settings
description: User wants the status line to refresh more frequently (around once per second) so recent transcriptions appear promptly. Investigate Claude Code settings.json statusLine options (refresh/padding) and bundle the appropriate config with the plugin where possible.
close_reason: Added refreshInterval: 1 to ~/.claude/settings.json statusLine (per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/statusline — not in the JSON schema but documented). Also audited statusline.fish: merged 4 session_json jq calls into 1 and 3 timer_status jq calls into 1, and cached the ss+curl timer probe 10s. Wall time per tick: ~160ms → ~120ms.

value: On this machine, default ALSA capture is near silence. Active mic for Converse tests is CORSAIR HS80 at CONVERSE_RECORDER_DEVICE=plughw:0,0; Dell monitor mic plughw:3,0 also has signal.
